method_note: Coverage judged against the candidate workspace-touching test set only (deterministic anchor). A test outside that set (e.g. plan-mode.service.test.ts, agent.service.test.ts, merge-cascade.test.ts) is NOT credited — noted in rationale where relevant. Strength=asserts-outcome means a test FAILS if the behaviour silently regresses. permission is near-N/A: single-user local-first, no RBAC; the one permission cell that matters is the merge readiness gate (not a role).

gap.rationale: The block (no second direct workspace row) is asserted, but the exact HTTP failure contract (4xx code vs error-in-201-body) is never asserted — the behaviour model flags this as an open unknown. A consumer relying on a specific status code is unguarded.

The turn route lives in workspace-actions.ts (churn 180, the single highest-churn workspace route file) — a refactor could silently drop the guard. Cheap negative test, regression-prone host file.

gap.rationale: No candidate test exercises the plan-gate HTTP surface: GET /:id/plan, POST /:id/implement-plan (201 → starts implementation), POST /:id/reject-plan with feedback (201 → re-plans), reject without feedback → 400. plan-mode.service.test.ts exists OUT of the candidate set (service logic only) and is not credited; the route gate that prevents an agent from writing code before approval is unverified end-to-end. AK-924 (plan-mode strand) is a recent regression in this exact area.

gap.rationale: No candidate test asserts that a zero-output-for-15-min agent is killed by the spawn-layer watchdog (reset on each output event). Implemented in agent.service.ts (churn 91 — the highest-churn workspace service file). agent.service.test.ts is outside the candidate set and not credited. Testable with fake timers; exec cost is moderate (must simulate the silent-stream timeout).

history.last_regression: ancestor-reconciler reaped fresh ws on hot-reload

gap.rationale: No candidate test asserts the restart-reattach invariant: live-PID sessions reattach + resume the output watcher from the last byte offset, dead-PID sessions are marked stopped and their workspaces reset to idle, agents are not killed on SIGTERM. High blast radius (whole-fleet survival), high churn host (agent.service.ts:509). Exec cost is high — requires simulating a server restart with a detached/unref'd PID.

history.last_regression: post-merge cascade leaked past manual Start Mode

behaviors.note: Covered by e2e-test-author run 2026-06-27 (P0). The test PINS start_mode=manual + dependency_auto_chain=false to isolate this followup path (gated by the auto_start_followup pref) from the SEPARATE maybeAutoStartUnblockedDependency path that produces an identical outcome — a dual-path confound caught by adversarial review. CORRECTION to the model: the gate is the auto_start_followup pref, NOT Start Mode (the requirements ref ADR-008:start-mode was wrong for this behaviour and is dropped). The multi-blocker partial-resolution boundary (every-guard) is NOT covered here — tracked as the new gap workspaces.cascade.partial-blockers-no-start in _priorities.md.

permission.note: near-N/A — single-user local-first, no RBAC; only the merge-readiness gate is a meaningful 'permission' cell

navigation.note: N/A for this capability — route/nav coverage belongs to the UI-navigation capability

accessibility.note: N/A — headless/API-first capability; a11y tracked at the component capability level

workspaces.score_note: (covered 20 + 0.5*partial 2) / 26 = 0.808. Updated 2026-06-27: e2e-test-author closed the P0 cascade.post-merge-followups gap (was the highest-risk uncovered), leaving reattach-survives-reload as the sole HIGH-risk uncovered. Remaining gaps cluster in hard-to-test runtime-lifecycle behaviours (watchdog, reattach) plus the new multi-blocker cascade boundary.

summary.weak_dimension_note: state-transition is the weakest column (4 uncovered + 1 partial) — every gap is a runtime lifecycle edge: plan approve/reject, hang-watchdog kill, restart-reattach, post-merge cascade, quarantine→In Progress. error is second-weakest, driven by two un-asserted negatives (turn-missing-content, one-direct exact status) and the watchdog. permission is correctly near-empty (single-user).
